steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Slope-intercept form is $f(x)=mx+b$ where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the y-intercept ($f(0)$).

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Given: slope $m=\frac{3}{4}$ and $f(0)=3$, so $b=3$. The function is $f(x)=\frac{3}{4}x+3$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Evaluate choices:
  • A ($f(x)=\frac{3}{4}x-3$): correct slope, wrong intercept ($-3$ instead of $3$) — eliminated.
  • B ($f(x)=\frac{3}{4}x+3$): slope $\frac{3}{4}$ and $f(0)=3$. B is correct.
  • C ($f(x)=4x-3$): slope is inverted and intercept is negative — eliminated.
  • D ($f(x)=4x+3$): slope is the reciprocal of $\frac{3}{4}$, not $\frac{3}{4}$ — eliminated.
